title: NA-ULIP
word_text:
Bajaj Life Opportunities Fund
The Bajaj Life Opportunities Fund is designed to capture high-potential growth across the Indian economy by identifying market-cap-agnostic opportunities. By combining a multi-engine approach with active stock selection, the fund aims to deliver superior risk-adjusted returns by pivoting between steady compounders and high-alpha special situations.
Note: Opportunities Fund will be available along with Bajaj Life ULIP Products.
Bajaj Life ULIP Products with underlying Opportunities Fund, enables policyholders to achieve their life goals, powered by the security of a life cover and the opportunity for wealth creation.
SFIN No: ULIF011406/02/26BLOPNLC250116
Investing post a period of correction can be rewarding
Nifty50 – 18-months rolling returns
Source :- NSE | Bloomberg I Bajaj Life Research I Data as on 27 Feb 2026
Nifty50 is currently flat on an 18-months basis.
This is a relatively infrequent occurrence and has happened a few times in the past 15-years due to specific reasons (COVID19, demonetization, policy paralysis etc.)
Interestingly, the experience of investing after such periods of correction has been fairly rewarding.
The average absolute returns over the next 5-years has been ~98% with the lowest return being 65%.
Market’s resilience in the face of War
Russia Ukraine War - 2022
Israel – Hamas War - 2023
Source :- NSE | Bloomberg I Bajaj Life Research
Despite the heightened geopolitical uncertainty in recent years, markets have been resilient and the impact of war has been short-lived.
The Russia-Ukraine War broke out in Feb-2022 and there were dire forecasts that the war would result in severe energy crisis (especially in Europe).
This transpired into a global risk-off sentiment which impacted India too with the Nifty50 correcting around ~7% in the immediate aftermath of the war.
However, the correction was short-lived and Nifty50 was much higher by the end of the year.
Similarly, the outbreak of the Israel-Hamas War in Oct-2023 resulted in an equity market correction with concerns surrounding a spike in crude oil prices.
Here again, market recouped its losses within months and was significantly higher 12-months later
Valuations of Large & Mid-Caps are trading lower to their long-term averages
Nifty 100 (Large Cap) : 1-Year Forward P/E
Nifty Mid Cap 150 : 1-Year Forward P/E
Source:- Bloomberg I Bajaj Life Research I Data as on 30 Jan 2026
The Nifty 100 (Large Cap) is trading at a 12-month forward P/E ratio of 20.43x, the valuations are marginally lower than its long-term average of 21.3 x.
Similarly, Nifty Mid Cap 150 (Mid Cap) is trading at a 12-month forward P/E ratio of 27.3x, the valuations are marginally higher than its long-term average of 27.1 x.
Why Bajaj Life Opportunities Fund?
Multi-Stage Growth Engine- Utilizes four distinct strategic engines to capture value from established leaders, emerging winners, and tactical cyclical shifts.
All-Season Appeal- Designed for long-term investors seeking a core equity holding that can adapt to different market cycles and volatility levels.
Diverse Opportunity Set- Strategically diversified across sectors and market caps, including special situations and mispriced turnaround ideas.
Expansive Investment Universe- Access to a vast universe of stocks across the Indian market, providing the flexibility to identify winners beyond standard index heavyweights. This broad reach allows the fund to remain agile, capitalizing on emerging alpha stories while maintaining a foundation in high-quality established businesses.
Market Cap Agnostic Strategy- Combines top-down macroeconomic analysis with rigorous bottom-up stock selection to identify the best opportunities regardless of company size. This flexible approach allows the fund to invest where growth potential is highest, shifting within market caps.
Investment Insight: The Indian opportunity story is evolving rapidly, driven by structural reforms and economic expansion. This fund is positioned to capture this transformation through a diversified, high-conviction portfolio that targets both consistent compounders and opportunistic alpha generators
Disciplined Portfolio Creation: From 700+ to 40-50* Best Ideas
Investment Philosophy
Focus on Growth at a Reasonable Price (GARP) with a long-term orientation.
We invest in businesses, not just stocks, seeking companies where opportunity size is substantial, and fundamentals are compelling.
*Depending upon the market condition, the number of stocks may vary
Bajaj Life Flexi Cap FundBajaj Life Flexi Cap FundOpportunities Fund: Four-Engine approach to finding alpha across market caps
Established Compounders (Large-Cap Leaders): Targets high-quality large companies with strong competitive moats, consistent cash flows, and visible earnings.
Emerging Winners (Mid-Cap Growth Stories): Mid-sized companies entering their high-growth phase or undergoing business transformation.
Cyclical & Thematic Opportunities: Companies benefiting from economic cycles, policy shifts, capex revival, or sectoral tailwinds.
Special Situations & Mispriced Ideas: Targets stock-specific price-to-fundamental disconnects where the best returns come from change-driven events.
Bajaj Life Recently Launched Active NFO
Bajaj Life Active Funds Performance Track Record Since Inception
Consistent outperformance: All five funds delivering positive alpha, demonstrating skilled active management and superior risk-adjusted returns across market capitalizations.
Performance data as on 30 Jan 2026 I Past performance is not indicative of future performance.
Is This Fund Right For You?
Long-Term Capital Growth- Suitable  for investors with a time horizon of five years and above, providing sufficient time to navigate market cycles and capture the full potential of high-growth investment themes.
Diversified Equity Exposure- Perfect for those seeking dynamic access to multiple market segments—from established large-cap leaders to emerging mid-cap winners—within a single, professionally managed portfolio.
Growth & Opportunity Theme- Designed for investors who want to capitalize on evolving market shifts, special situations, and mispriced ideas that offer superior return potential beyond traditional index heavyweights.
Risk-Agile Profile- Suited for investors with a high-risk appetite who are comfortable with equity volatility and seek to benefit from active management that targets both tactical cycles and structural transformations.
Bajaj Life Opportunities Fund
Investment Objective-  To achieve long-term capital appreciation by investing in a diversified basket of equity and equity related instruments of companies predominantly* from the top 250 companies by market capitalization.
*Minimum 60% of invested equity to be in large & midcap stocks
Benchmark Index- Nifty Large Mid Cap 250
Risk Classification- Very High Risk: Investors should be prepared for significant volatility and potential capital loss. Suitable for investors with high risk tolerance, long term investment horizons.
Strategic Framework for Sector Preference
The Bajaj Life Opportunities Fund leverages 4-engine framework to identify high-potential investment opportunities and sector preferences:
Disciplined & Dynamic Allocation: We utilize a disciplined sector allocation approach with the flexibility to adjust focus across sectors as market conditions evolve.
Optimal Positioning: Our dynamic approach ensures optimal positioning for long-term value creation by capturing emerging opportunities and aligning preferences with evolving consumer trends.
Strategic Focus: Current positioning emphasizes sectors that offer a balance of near-term catalysts and long-term structural growth, specifically targeting those with:
Strong earnings visibility
Reasonable valuations
Sustainable competitive advantages
Disclaimer
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Past performance  of a fund is not necessarily indicative of the future performance of the fund.
Please note that the fund aims to replicate the performance of benchmark index fund, subject to tracking error.
